The First Lantern was lit beneath the river shrine when the valley had no sun.
Elders say the moon bell was forged from silver pulled out of a fallen star.
The ruined watchtower marks the border between the old crown road and the thorn wood.
Every midsummer, the village leaves bread and salt at the sealed north gate.
The blue flame under the well is older than the keep and colder than winter rain.
Ironwood trees grow only where a promise was broken and buried.
The river stone remembers every oath spoken beside running water.
When the shrine wakes, its doors open inward to a room full of daylight.
